entry.title: Re: CAP Expand query does not function
entry.content: Have you tried to add an await?

entry.title: Re: Tell us what guides you'd like to have GitHub enabled
entry.content:
Hi @je-hal 

Thank you for your response. That's too bad - I hoped open documentation initiative and improvement proposals with GitHub pull request would be enabled throughout the entire SAP Help documentation. I personally like GitHub and think it is a very straight forward, clean process to contribute to SAP documentation. 

Anyway, comments would be a viable option too, if they were enabled for business applications documentation. Unfortunately, I can't find them on S/4HANA or ECC help pages. Probably, as you said, the comments are not enabled there. 

Do you have any process to request comments enablement on particular help pages? I'm sure SAP Help could benefit a lot from users feedback. Right now, there is no way to post one.

Cheers

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Save the Date for Our Exclusive SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series!
entry.content:
With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, we offer a fully-fledged cloud ERP that optimizes processes and helps your business to run smoothly. We as the Cloud ERP PSCC organization strive to enable our valued customers and partners to take full advantage of the benefits our solution brings for the intelligent, sustainable enterprise.

Therefore, we offer a variety of enablement assets to explain the features and functions of each new release and make sure that you get the maximum value out of your investment.

The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Early Release Series is a series of compelling live product expert sessions that takes place twice a year and is designed by product experts for product experts to give customers, partners, and colleagues a special insider’s preview into the highlights for selected lines of business and industries, 1-2 weeks before the actual release. In a span of multiple days, we give you the opportunity to see the latest demos, ask your questions, and get answers straight from the product experts before everyone else.

In July 2024, we will be back with a set of compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you.

🗓️ Don't miss this chance and save the date!

Stay tuned for the complete agenda and registration links to follow.

Find the recordings of previous live sessions here, here, and here.

How to Sign Up for the SAP Enterprise Support Edition as a Customer/Partner

In order to sign up for the online sessions, you will need to have an active SAP Learning Hub license. While the series is available across all editions, you can find the steps below how to register for the SAP Enterprise Support edition in case you don’t already have access to any of the editions yet.

  • Navigate to the sign up page 
  • Click the Sign up button. Authenticate yourself with your s-user ID and password. Upon first access, the system will check your eligibility, create a new SAP Learning Hub user, and populate your learning catalog respectively. Within two hours, you will then receive a registration confirmation via e-mail.
  • After you have received the confirmation e-mail, the registration process is complete.

 

How to Sign Up for the Internal Edition as an SAP Employee

In order to sign up for the online sessions, you will need to have an SAP Learning Hub license. In case you have no edition assigned to your profile, please register here for the Internal Edition which is free of charge.

Make sure you be logged onto the SAP Learning Hub before opening the URL. In case the link doesn't open, copy and paste it into your browser. If you face any other issues with your login, please contact the help center.

Follow the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition tag and the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with the latest blog posts. 

Follow us on @Sap and #S4HANA, and myself on here, LinkedIn, and X.

 

🎙️ Inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud

There is no customer success without project success and product success! Leverage the unique knowledge and expertise of SAP S/4HANA Cloud experts, partners, and customers, sharing their SAP S/4HANA Cloud implementation project best practices and lessons learned – anywhere, anytime. In this podcast, we give you all ingredients to get to the next level and make your SAP S/4HANA Cloud project a success. Subscribe now and benefit from the shared knowledge!

 

💻openSAP Microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Our openSAP microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA Cloud offer an exciting new learning opportunity. What began with a small batch of 20 videos, has now become a channel with more than 50 microlearnings that have generated over 20,000 views. Today, we cover multiple lines of business such as finance, manufacturing, and supply chain management, and key technology topics like Master Data Management, extensibility, SAP User Experience, and upgrade management. We are continuously adding new microlearnings to our channel, so make sure you check them out. 

 

🗣️ Your Voice Matters

You want to learn more and actively engage with SAP subject matter experts on SAP S/4HANA Cloud topics? We have just the right place for you! Join our interactive community where we bring together customers, partners, and SAP experts with a clear mission: To engage with one another about best practices and product solutions. We invite you to explore the ‘one-stop shop’ as the central place for all resources, tools, content questions, answers and to connect with experts who will guide you through your journey to the intelligent, sustainable enterprise.

 

🔎Find All Our Assets

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

 

🎯Where to Find More Information:

  • Read up on the highlights of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 release here: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/highlights-of-the-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-2402-release/ba-p/13587480
  • Interested in an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 update on the intelligent ERP? Read all about it here: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-2402-intelligent-erp-update/ba-p/13584760 
  • Watch the replays of our exclusive SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 Early Release Series here: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/watch-the-replays-of-our-exclusive-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-2402/ba-p/13587019
  • Explore the ready-to-run cloud ERP here: https://www.sap.com/products/erp/s4hana.html
  • Watch our SAP S/4HANA Cloud product update videos on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDnnyN2j-CcUheNN-GaNCb3H
  • Watch our SAP Expert Talks here: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDl0m4Wlj7ttWqA1on5oxwvc 
  • Check out our digital enablement wheel here: https://chart-bdmaicr0au.dispatcher.eu2.hana.ondemand.com/index.html?hc_reset
  • Find out how to switch from groups to spaces and pages here: https://blogs.sap.com/2023/11/06/deactivation-of-groups-in-the-sap-fiori-launchpad-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-2402-switching-to-spaces-and-pages-mandatory/
  • Listen to our podcast here: https://podcast.opensap.info/inside-sap/
  • Watch our openSAP microlearnings to learn about SAP S/4HANA Cloud topics at your own pace here: https://microlearning.opensap.com/
  • Find best practices on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition here: https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-013/latest?region=DE
  • Check out what’s new here: https://help.sap.com/doc/ce01d82756b947a1a043a5d5a3204226  
  • Find help here: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD
  • Read the feature scope description here: https://help.sap.com/doc/7c9e0bbbd1664c2581b2038a1c7ae4b3

entry.title: Anyway within LMS to product list of Content Objects that have 'Enable Mobile Access' on/off?
entry.content: SuccessFactors Learning - Anyway within LMS to product list of Content Objects that have 'Enable Mobile Access' on/off?

entry.title: Re: Characteristics relation is not populating
entry.content:
Hi,

your relation of type exist is not of type 'derivation' (cf. the respective flag in the definition of a relation in RSPLAN), this is why the system only calls the method CHECK (and CREATE, depending on the context). Create a relation of type derivation where you specify source and target characteristics. Method DERIVE is then called when all source characteristics are known (or where 'derived' in relations in previous steps) and at least one target characteristic is not known (i.e. is filled).

Regards,

Gregor

entry.title: Re: SIGNAVIO Governance: Start a workflow from dictionary...
entry.content: Tks Cacilda!! I hope these feature can help many clients too.. Not only for start a Process Governance from dictionary elements but from Colllaboration Hub too

entry.title: Re: Is there a transaction to delete an article (Material Number) from SAP?
entry.content:
First you have Discontinue the Article via tcode WRF_Dis_Sel' then next SARA tocde select the ranges for archival process to complete

 

SAP Retail Article Deletion FMCG #Retail #Article Deletion

entry.title: Re: Mass Reversals or Mass Void of checks in S/4HANA Cloud

Hi mbilodeau

Have you looked into the app Manage Checks (https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/index.html#/detail/Apps('FPCHR')/S32)

Seems there are a lot of selection options there. You can even void an entire payment run.

Thank you
Jerry

entry.title: Re: CAP Expand query does not function
entry.content: @gregorw I double checked the await command after your reply. I already use await during the request handling, but it ends with an empty array nevertheless. const characteristicCatalogSetResult = await this.tx(request).run(characteristicCatalogQuery); I tried to add the await on the query preparation (Select statement), but in that case ended with an error.

entry.title: Re: Mass Reversals or Mass Void of checks in S/4HANA Cloud

I'm looking at a process to void staledated checks (checks that have not been cashed and have been pending for a long time. (NB) We do not have the scope item 2DP and we are also on Public Cloud. This link seems more appropriate to us https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/index.html#/detail/Apps('FPCHR')/S32 for us but still refers app Check Management that only seem to be included in scope item 2DP. Is activating scope item 2DP the only options, you think?

entry.title: Navigating the Storm: Overcoming the Challenges of Business Process Management Implementation
entry.content:
Photo by Torsten Dederichs on Unsplash

The implementation of Business Process Management (BPM) can offer impressive benefits to a business, from increased productivity to improved efficiency and error reduction. However, getting started with BPM does present challenges and can often feel like setting sail into uncharted waters. For those spearheading this endeavor, challenges in the early stages are not uncommon. With the right strategies and mindset, these hurdles can be overcome, paving the way for smoother sailing ahead. 

In this blog, we’ll explore some key challenges faced during the initial phases of a BPM implementation and provide actionable insights on how to navigate them effectively.

Problem: Lack of Clear Vision and Strategy 

One of the most common stumbling blocks in BPM implementation is the absence of a clear vision and strategy. Without a roadmap, it’s easy to lose direction and encounter roadblocks along the way. 

Solution: 

To overcome this challenge, start by defining your organization’s objectives and desired outcomes from BPM. Engage stakeholders in the process to ensure alignment and buy-in. Develop a comprehensive strategy that outlines key milestones, timelines, and resource allocations.

Problem: Resistance to Change

Implementing BPM often involves significant changes to existing processes, roles, and responsibilities. Resistance to these changes can arise from employees who are comfortable with the status quo or fear disruption to their workflows. 

Solution:

To address this challenge, communication and training are essential. Keep employees informed about the reasons behind BPM implementation and how it will benefit them and the organization as a whole. Provide training and support to help them adapt to new processes and tools.

Problem: Integration Issues

Integrating BPM software with existing systems and technologies can be complex, especially in organizations with disparate IT infrastructures. Integration issues can lead to data silos, inefficiencies, and inconsistencies in process execution. 

Solution:

To tackle this challenge, take a phased approach to integration, starting with high-priority processes or departments. Work closely with IT teams and vendors to ensure seamless integration and data flow between systems. Conduct thorough testing to identify and resolve any integration issues early on.

Problem: Lack of Executive Sponsorship

Without strong executive sponsorship, BPM initiatives may struggle to gain traction and support across the organization. Executive sponsors play a crucial role in championing BPM, securing resources, and driving alignment with strategic goals. 

Solution:

To overcome this challenge, identify and engage executive sponsors who understand the value of BPM and are committed to its success. Build a compelling business case that highlights the potential ROI and benefits of BPM implementation.

Problem: Measuring Success

Effective measurement and monitoring are essential for assessing the impact of BPM initiatives and identifying areas for improvement. However, defining meaningful metrics and KPIs can be challenging, especially in the early stages of implementation. 

Solution

To address this challenge, start by identifying key performance indicators that align with your organization’s objectives and desired outcomes. Establish baseline metrics to track progress over time and make data-driven decisions. Regularly review and refine your measurement framework based on feedback and evolving business needs.

These are just five (5) of the common challenges in the early stages of a BPM implementation. BUT the biggest problem in most organizations is usually Impatience. 

It is important to remember that BPM isn’t a silver bullet and will not effect change overnight. It is a long-term commitment and requires patience. There may (will) be pressure, especially from upper management, to see immediate results. It’s essential to manage expectations and remind all involved that real, substantive progress will take time.

As we have discussed, BPM implementations do require careful planning, communication, and collaboration across the organization. By addressing common hurdles head-on and leveraging the right strategies and tools, analysts and managers can avoid shipwreck and lay a solid foundation for successful a BPM adoption and drive long-term business value.

Remember, Patience! You are on a journey not a sprint. While it may be challenging, the rewards of effective BPM implementation are well worth the effort.

— — — — — — — — — — — -

If you found this article helpful, be sure to click like. And course follow me for more on the topics of AI, BPM, Process Mining, Customer Experience, Digital Transformation, and Automation.

Until next time — Keep Transforming 💡

entry.title: Re: CR or Data Slice can be implemented with Workbook ID?
entry.content:
Hi,

characteristic relationships (CRs) by design do not depend on queries or workbooks as a CR is a concept to define the consistent data records. Queries and workbooks (using queries) are only views on the transaction data, all views should have the same consistency model.

If the queries are working on disjoint data that can be separated by a 'separating' characteristic (like version or a time characteristic) make these characteristics part of you CRs; another option is to separate the data and thus the relations using different part providers in a composite provider.

Data slices can be deactivated temporarily in planning sequences (e.g. for administrative tasks); of end users data slice are usually parameterized using variables. You can also check the 'work status' concept in BPC Embeded.

Regards,

Gregor

entry.title: Re: Draft Report with Bin Locations - Goods Receipt PO
entry.content: Basically yeah. I have not looked specifically at your query but what I'm seeing in your example is the UNION ALL. Trying to merge the real and draft documents into a single table structure is confusing (to me) so my approach would be a UNION. Does this query give you the information you need? Or are you still having issues with the OPDN / BIN links?

entry.title: Re: In Rewards and recognition we need to hide below thin...
entry.content: It's not possible to hide these items.

entry.title: Re: Trend in compensation (Story reports)
entry.content: This question should be tagged to the Reporting module, not the compensation module.

entry.title: Complete Execution Button Reversal
entry.content:
Can I perform a reversal once a click on the Complete Execution?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Complete Execution Button Reversal
entry.content:
Once the user clicks on the Complete Execution button under the Items tab, there a two possible outcomes: 

  1. Click Close button.
  2. A Confirmation Dialog will open.
  3. Select No.

This will close the document without saving the change performed.

Or

  1. Save the document and the line item is completed. 

Once the button is clicked and saved, no reversal can be performed as this is a confirmation that the Delivery was finished.

entry.title: Re: HRA pay component value as amount .
entry.content: It looks like this question should be tagged as Employee Central, not as Compensation.

entry.title: Feasibility of Creating a Single PO for Multiple Business Units
entry.content:
Dear All,

We are exploring the possibility of creating a single Purchase Order (PO) for a supplier, encompassing all our business units located in different countries.

I am aware that there are variations in tax regulations and delivery locations. However, our aim is to streamline the process with one consolidated PO.

Could you please advise if this is feasible within SAP? If so, what key factors should we consider before proceeding with this implementation?

Thank you in advance for your assistance.

SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain Sourcing and Procurement - Purchase Contract Management MM Purchasing

Many thanks in advance

entry.title: External cash desk posting with payment form reference
entry.content:
Hello,

We are using external cash desk for posting customer collection. These collections are posted to customer account real time and open item is also getting cleared. We have one more requirement wherein when we receive payment it has to be posted with reference to payment form and should clear relevant line items only which are attached to payment form.

Currently we are using contract account document as exterenal cash desk reference and payment is posted successfully with clearing. But due to change in business process we want to post and clear collection with payment form, which is not happening. It is not allowing to post and if it is posted then it is not clearing the line items. 

Please suggest how to use payment form reference id for external cash desk posting and clearing.

Regards,

Narendra

entry.title: Re: Move Checkbox UDF to Mainform via UI Configuration
entry.content: I have the same problem, it doesn't work. Can't find a workaround.

entry.title: Schedule Lines not getting deleted when passing Delete Indicator to BAPI : BAPI_SCHEDULE_MAINTAIN
entry.content:
Issue:

  • Whenever we are trying deletion of a schedule line , the custom program is passing on the input to BAPI : BAPI_SCHEDULE_MAINTAIN with the Scheduling Agreement Number , Item Number and Schedule line and Delete Indicator as ‘X’ to the BAPI.

  •  

    However when this input is sent to BAPI , it is only changing the Quantity to ‘0.00’ for that schedule line and marking the line as fixed and not deleting it.
  • We have observed this is only happening for those Scheduling agreements where releases were generated before by ME84.

Does anyone knows why the lines are not getting deleted and how this can be resolved?

entry.title: Re: Creating an SAP Account and getting started in the community
entry.content: It was a lovely start in sap , hoping for best learning experience in my life. Im intereted in learning and getting certified in SAP modules like SD, BI , FICO and more.

entry.title: Re: Mass Reversals or Mass Void of checks in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi mbilodeau

I apologize - I did inadvertently copy the wrong link but I am glad you found the public cloud one.

Unfortunately, I am not 100% certain if that app is limited to only contract accounting.

Here is the help link, which is under the Contract Accounting section:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/cdccca8e03d74101a0135863bc522b49/ab0bc5536a51204be10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

Let me provide a couple screenshots of the filter options

The Custom Selections seems fairly extensive

Hopefully someone else here will have some better insight. Otherwise, I would recommend to see if you could identify a consultant with the knowledge.

I wouldn't want to recommend to activate 2DP if it doesn't help.

entry.title: Re: Draft Report with Bin Locations - Goods Receipt PO
entry.content: So when you run your draft side of the query it returns no results? If it returns no results then the UNION won't fix that part.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Fully-Activated Appliance: Demo Guides
Re: Cost Centre Segreggation
entry.content:
Hello Rishabh, 

You can create additional hierarchy for cost center reporting and group relevant cost centers. Use KSH1 to create hierarchy and group cost centers.

regards

Narendra

entry.title: "Error Getting Function" during RFC connection in CPI
entry.content:
I've created a Z function and I've tried to call it from CPI but an error always occur:

It is correctly exposed in CC using the RFC name but it would also be available since all Z RFCs are accessible using prefix:

We also have many other Z RFCs being called without any issue. 

I've tried changing the request payload -- even copying from sproxy the sample request -- but nothing changes. I also have found a couple post from the SAP Community but none of them gave me actual solution. Please advise. Thank you in advance

 

Error Details
com.sap.it.rt.adapter.http.api.exception.HttpResponseException: An internal server error occured: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="no"?><rfc:ZRFC_XXXXX.Exception xmlns:rfc="urn:sap-com:document:sap:rfc:functions"><Exceptions> <E1>Error Getting Function</E1> </Exceptions></rfc:ZRFC_XXXXX.Exception>. The MPL ID for the failed message is : AGZaBeDsJhAGLWrtFEOXXXXXX

entry.title: Re: Mass Reversals or Mass Void of checks in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content: Yes, it is an internal test system with 2DP activated. It's a fairly new system with not a lot of test data in at the moment. It don't have any checks in the system yet that I could try to void. What scope item are you working in (I could potentially try to follow the test script to do a test).

entry.title: Error trying to use master form SOMU_FORM_MASTER_A4
entry.content:
I'm trying to create a new purchase order form using the template MM_PUR_PURCHASE_ORDER and use the SOMU_FORM_MASTER_A4 as the master but it doesn't load correctly. I can't see the fragments and it's not greyed out like its supposed to be. (image attached)

I've downloaded it and extracted everything to a folder, opened on Adobe Livecycle Designer and this is how it looks (image attached)

How do I get all the fragments to appear with the correct options?

entry.title: Error 'This process is unavailable or is configured incorrectly' in My Inbox app (SAP ECC)
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I need your help with one of the issue in facing in configuring My Inbox app. I have configured Process Inbox by following Implementation Documentation from FIORI App Library.

I have a PO Approval workflow which I am able to see in my My Inbox app as well but when I am clicking on Open Task button, I am getting error page with 'This process is unavailable or is configured incorrectly' message.

Kindly help me with the process to display PO details in My Inbox app.

Environment

  • GBX01HR5
  • UIHR002

Kanchana

entry.title: Error assigning users to roles in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
We are facing an error in SAP Cloud ALM of the customer.

Once we try to assign users into roles in SAP Cloud ALM, this error appears: An error occurred while updating roles for User XXXXXXX.

The users has been created in IAS and they are in "active" status there.

This error is to asign the role "project member" to the users.

I press the "save" and the following message appears:

I tried to find support on this error, but I can´t find anything.

The project will start next week and it is quite urgent.

Any good answer?

Many thanks!

entry.title: Re: Mass Reversals or Mass Void of checks in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content: Hi, it would be under scope Item J60, thansk!

entry.title: FRML002 Overflow during conversion in field error in Recipe entry
entry.content:
We are getting an FRML002 Overflow during conversion in field error when we enter a quantity of 1000 KG or more.  

Unfortunately  I have not been able to trace back to where the error is invoked, or to determine how to resolve this and would appreciate any assistance with this.

Thanks in advance for your responses

entry.title: Partial Service Order Confirmation
entry.content:
Hello All,

At a client they have a need to invoice a service order before all confirmations are completed (multiple invoices throughout the lifecycle of a service order).  In the starter system it is not allowing our team to do this, it only allows us to release for billing once a 'final confirmation' is completed.  Can anyone help point me in the right direction?

#servicemanagement

Thanks, JG

entry.title: SAP B1 DOES NOT ALLOW ME TO CREATE NEW COMPANY
entry.content: I am trying to create a new company in SAP B1 but it always gives me this error, I already used several names without spaces or symbols and the error still persists. How could I solve it?

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports: "maximum report processing job limit"
entry.content:
CR for VS are full builds, SAP doesn't "patch" old versions.

I suggest moving to SP 35 from here:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354091173.html

See my blog for upgrading:

Upgrading a Visual Studio 2008->2022 .NET project with the latest CR .NET SDK packages

As I suggested you'll have to debug the runtime using ProcessMonitor from Microsoft. Compare the runtime and registry keys. Run your app on your DEV PC and then run it on the Prod server and compare the logs files.

Make sure you are using the same Runtime on your DEV PC as Production, you can't mix versions.

And add logging component to your app so you can see what's happening.

I was sure Dell added a response to this post on how to manage Report Objects and all objects opened, close them once they are completed, including any Data objects etc. CR will log off the DB once the report is closed.

You'll have to do more debugging, any else I suggest is just guesswork...

One of my old SAP co-workers always liked to say "you are the only one having this issue, if more had the same problem it would be easy to fix, something you are doing is not following the proper workflow..."

Don

entry.title: A very strange SQL requirement
entry.content:
Hello, we have the following 2 tables: ZEK_SBOOK & ZEK_SCARR with the following data:

CARRIDFLDATENAMESEATS
AA30.05.2024ELIAS5
AB30.05.2024MARIA2
AA31.05.2024JOHN3

 

CARRIDAIR_NAME
AAAIRLINE1
ABAIRLINE2
AFAIRLINE3
BEAIRLINE4

What the customer is asking is to have the following ALV.

CARRIDFLDATENAME
AA30.05.2024ELIAS
AB30.05.2024MARIA
AF30.05.2024 
BE30.05.2024 
AA31.05.2024JOHN
AB31.05.2024 
AF31.05.2024 
BE31.05.2024 

In a few words they want to have all airlines for each date with the data of the table ZEK_SBOOK having or not.

Is it possible to do it in a query?

Thanks

Elias

entry.title: Re: Deploy failed. Detail: Cannot read properties of unde...
entry.content:
@devangelgb @sapdude I couldn't reproduce this issue on my side. We are not quite sure what has triggered this.  

If you experience this error again, please do the following and share the details:

  • Check if the .build/uploadBundle.zip is generated in the project explorer, download it locally by right-clicking on it. Navigate to mobile services admin UI > App configuration > app update > upload to mobile services manually to see if upload works.
  • Check Logs (Mobile Services admin UI > Analytics > Logs) if there is any related information

entry.title: Re: How to rename cell Totals - SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi jain06,
Thank you for your feedback.
This feature does indeed work. The only constraint is that when you check the formula bar, the column total will be displayed (see screenshot).

I was looking for a way around this problem.

Regards,

entry.title: Empower Your Enterprise: SAP BRIM Explained
entry.content:
During the SAP Inside Track Kolkata 2024 event, the renowned speaker Swapna Borra captivated the audience with an extraordinary live session on SAP BRIM – Entitlement Management. Her presentation was nothing short of phenomenal, delivering profound insights and showcasing the robust capabilities and advantages of SAP BRIM in entitlement management. The session left an indelible mark on the attendees, underscoring the transformative potential of this technology.

Optimizing Entitlement Management with SAP BRIM

SAP BRIM (Billing and Revenue Innovation Management), previously known as SAP Hybris Billing, is a comprehensive billing solution designed for industries with high-volume consumption. It simplifies the intricate billing and invoicing processes, supporting flexible consumption models. SAP BRIM facilitates a range of flexible consumption models. As a modular solution, it is crafted to enhance the business lifecycle processes, including design, sales, delivery, and billing. This versatile solution is utilized across various sectors, including telecommunications, financial services, utilities, media, logistics, and public transport.

SAP BRIM Components

The solution comprises four core components:

  • SAP BRIM, subscription order management (SOM)
  • SAP Convergent Charging (CC)
  • SAP Convergent Invoicing (CI)
  • SAP Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable (FI-CA)

Collectively, these components assist in overseeing business model design, executing order management, monitoring usage metering and transaction pricing, handling billing and invoice processing, managing receivables and payables, royalties, and commissions, and offering support for financial customer care.

Some of the Use Cases across Industries:

Features of SAP BRIM:

Difference between the version of SAP BRIM Solution Architecture:

The components of the SAP BRIM solution are available both as integrated areas within SAP S/4HANA and as standalone modules that seamlessly interface with SAP S/4HANA.

The diagram below demonstrates how each core component of SAP BRIM fits within the end-to-end solution landscape. SAP BRIM and subscription order management, along with SAP Convergent Charging, function as standalone applications and are deployed in separate instances, indicated by (1) for SAP BRIM and subscription order management, and (2) for SAP Convergent Charging. Conversely, SAP Convergent Invoicing and FI-CA are integral parts of the SAP S/4HANA solution, included by default with SAP S/4HANA. These are represented in the diagram with (3), corresponding to SAP S/4HANA, FI-CA, and SAP Convergent Invoicing.

Fig. SAP BRIM Older Version – 2018

In the new architecture of SAP S/4HANA 1909, subscription order management has been integrated into the SAP S/4HANA solution, as shown below. This integration greatly simplifies the implementation and integration processes for companies adopting the SAP BRIM solution.

 

Fig. SAP BRIM New version

Subscription Order Management (SOM)

In the latest version of S4/HANA, Subscription Order Management (SOM) has become a crucial component of the comprehensive BRIM solution. This enhancement is an additional function built upon SAP CRM. Previously, SAP CRM served as the primary interface for businesses to interact with customers for service and product selection.

SAP SOM and CRM serve as the starting point for SAP BRIM, managing the entire product catalog, orders, distribution creation, and contract lifecycle management. It is implemented with Advanced Variant Configuration (AVC) to allow for flexible product configuration. Once contracts and orders are established in SOM, they are replicated to SAP FI-CA and SAP Convergent Charging for further processes, such as invoicing, billing, charging, and rating.

Fig. Subscription Order Management

SAP BRIM, with its subscription order management module, enables companies to efficiently manage their customers through a range of specialized features designed to flexibly meet customer needs. These features include:

  • Product catalog and offer management.
  • Order capture
  • Order distribution
  • Contract management
  • Customer care

 

     

 

Convergent Charging (CC)

SAP Convergent Charging is engineered to provide pricing flexibility for customer charging. Within SAP Convergent Charging, a charge refers to the price derived from a rating and charging event, which is billed to the customer (or subscriber). Essentially, SAP Convergent Charging acts as a calculation engine, determining various types of charges, including recurring, one-time, and usage-based fees.

Fig. Convergent Charging

The figure above depicts the integration of SAP Convergent Charging within the SAP BRIM solution. SAP Convergent Charging consists of two primary components: charging and rating. Charging is the process by which SAP Convergent Charging identifies the specific customer account to be billed. Conversely, rating involves the calculation method that determines the total amount to be charged in the billing document.

Convergent Invoicing (CI)

Convergent Invoicing (CI) offers a versatile solution for automating billing and invoicing processes. It begins by receiving inputs (such as complete bills, recurring charges, or rated events) from one or multiple billing or rating systems. These inputs are stored based on their status and consolidated into a single invoice for consumers, along with an individual outstanding receivable.

Fig. Convergent Invoicing

SAP CI seamlessly integrates multiple billing sources into a unified, streamlined invoice from various origins. Once the rated and charged items enter SAP CI, they are stored as billable products, and the processes of billable item aggregation, invoice creation, and billing are carried out efficiently.

Contract Account Receivable and Payable (FI-CA)

This is the final fundamental component of SAP BRIM handles financial documents, credits and collections, dispute management, and payment processing. SAP Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable (FI-CA) serves as the financial backend, providing sub-ledger accounting tailored to meet the needs of industries that engage with numerous customers and transactions. FI-CA offers a comprehensive suite of features, including tax calculations and compliance with country-specific procedures such as partner settlement, collection management, payment processing, and various accounting principles.

Fig. Contract Accounting

SAP Entitlement Management

SAP Entitlement Management simplifies and automates entitlement processes for companies, efficiently managing the entire entitlement lifecycle (i.e., overseeing what customers are entitled to). This solution ensures clear visibility for both customers and sales representatives, providing alerts, reminders, and other notifications. As a result, customer satisfaction is enhanced, and opportunities for upselling and cross-selling are significantly increased.

Fig. Entitlement Management Architecture

Fig. Entitlement Management – Use Cases

Conclusion:

SAP BRIM is a robust solution designed for managing subscriptions and other recurring revenue streams. At a high level, the process begins with order creation in SAP BRIM's subscription order management. Calculations are then performed in SAP Convergent Charging based on usage, resulting in the final charge amounts. Bills and invoices are generated and sent to customers via SAP Convergent Invoicing. Finally, in contract accounting, financial documents are posted, and payments are received or made. Contract accounting also facilitates dispute management, credit management, and collections.

 

@DataTherapist 
@Swapnaborra

entry.title: Re: Clean Core Approach for SAP Fiori Development
entry.content:
I personally think "Clean Core" is  a buzz word made up to impress an audience of executives who don't really know what happens during development.

We always did clean core development, even in the traditional enhancements whenever we created an extension or a Z object, it would always be created "on the side", the core never got touched anyways.   

All of those methods you mentioned, and everything else we as developers did to extend a standard program are clean core, in my opinion.  

I don't see anything drastically different now. Even key-user UI adaptation existed in Web Dynpro in FPM customization layer. 

Just mho.

entry.title: Re: Good to know: Firefighter Session related changes from Access Control Service Pack 21
entry.content:
Mate, thanks for creating this blog. After upgrading to FNDA SP21 on the central GRC system we are seeing issues with FF session windows not opening on the satellite system when pressing the logon button in /grcpi/gria_eam in decentralized FF model and observed this behavior when attempting to launch the FF session. 

  1. FFxxxxx user was unlocked in the satellite system (decentralized)
  2. first attempt got "max user sessions" error in SAP GUI, FF session window not opened
  3. clicked logoff in /grcpi/gria_eam
  4. FFxxxxx user now locked
  5. second attempt no session window opened
  6. FFxxxxx remains locked

    SAP support has asked to check the RFC to ensure the system information and RFC destinations match and they do. Still problem is occurring. Has anyone else ran into something similar?

entry.title: How to execute an action on scroll UI5 page control to top
entry.content:
Hi.

How to execute a acction o scrll UI5 page control on top. I would like to call a function, when I scroll a page control to top. I have tried with DomRef and ScrollContainer, but I haven't get success. So who can say me how to do that?

Thanks and best regards.

entry.title: FMDERIVE in MIRO issue
entry.content:
Hello dear Experts!

We have implemented the derivation step in FMDERIVE to change the commitment item while posting the subsequent debit in MIRO against PO. This step successfully changes the commitment item in FI-document, FM-document, but not in the MM-document (it has the old value inherited from PO). The result looks like inconsistency.

We use S/4HANA2020.

Does anybody have any ideas how to fix it?

entry.content:
Great Blog, any steps on how to take it further on Fiori Apps? for example below app ..

ID F3417 Gross Margin - Presumed/Actual

entry.title: Re: Selection screen fields in SAP Query
entry.content:
Thank you, this works, however it removes SELECTION BLOCKS. Which the users do not like.
I'm working on another solution. If you have an enhancement to this process, please share. I will share when the solution is found.

entry.title: Re: [SAP MDK] Saving data in a GLOBAL variable/context an...
entry.content:
Tausend Dank Bill  🙏

For those encountering the same "challenge", here's a short/direct guide:

  • To create and set a new property (variable) -here "NewProp"- within the "global container" ClientData , you would write the following:

 

context.getPageProxy().getClientData().NewProp = "New"​​

 

  • To read the value from this newly defined property, use:

 

let newLocalVar = context.getPageProxy().getClientData().NewProp;​

entry.title: SAP VCH AFL Installation
entry.content:
Hello Network,

We are looking for VCH AFL Installation,

This is our HANA version 2.00.067.04.1700654562 (fa/hana2sp06),

According to available blogs, our Revision is 067,

Our Hana Application Sever is 2022,

We can't locate the compatible VCH AFL to our application as revision 067 is not available,

We tried HANA 2.0 Rev 67.04 but it's not working as well as the HANA 2.0 Rev 67,

https://me.sap.com/softwarecenter/template/products/_APP=00200682500000001943&_EVENT=NEXT&HEADER=Y&FUNCTIONBAR=Y&EVENT=TREE&NE=NAVIGATE&ENR=73554900100800000266&V=MAINT&TA=ACTUAL/SAP%20S4HANA

entry.title: Re: ONB 2.0 Documents Integration to OpenText
entry.content: This blog is very informative. It seems to only reference sending documents to Opentext if an onboardee has attached / uploaded documents during the onboarding process. Does anyone know what is needed to send documents that have been electronically signed (polices that have been e-signed for example) as part of the ONB 2.0 process?  We don't have onboardees uploading documents but they are clicking to sign our company policies and those are the documents we want to integrate to OpenText.

entry.title: Leveraging SAP AI Core APIs to Build your own AI Powered Apps
entry.content:
SAP AI Core is a service in the SAP Business Technology Platform that is designed to handle the execution and operations of your AI assets in a standardized, scalable, and hyperscaler-agnostic way. It provides seamless integration with your SAP solutions. Any AI function can be easily realized using open-source frameworks. SAP AI Core supports full lifecycle management of AI scenarios.

AI Core API Documentation

Use the below template to your Visual Studio Code

 

###
#  login
GET {{auth}}/oauth/token?grant_type=client_credentials
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Basic {{clientid}}:{{clientsecret}}


@access_token = {{login.response.body.access_token}}


###
GET {{serviceurl}}/admin/resourceGroups
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{access_token}}

###
GET {{serviceurl}}/admin/services
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{access_token}}

###
GET {{serviceurl}}/lm/scenarios
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{access_token}}
AI-Resource-Group: default

###
GET {{serviceurl}}/lm/healthz
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{access_token}}


###
GET {{serviceurl}}/admin/resourceQuota/resourceGroups
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{access_token}}

###
GET {{serviceurl}}/admin/resourceQuota/executables
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{access_token}}

###
GET {{serviceurl}}/admin/resourceQuota/applications
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{access_token}}

###
GET {{serviceurl}}/admin/resourceQuota/repositories
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{access_token}}


###
GET {{serviceurl}}/admin/resourceQuota/secrets
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{access_token}}


###
GET {{serviceurl}}/admin/resourceQuota/dockerRegistrySecrets
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{access_token}}

###
GET {{serviceurl}}/admin/applications
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{access_token}}

###
GET {{serviceurl}}/admin/repositories
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{access_token}}

###
GET {{serviceurl}}/admin/resources/nodes
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{access_token}}

###
GET {{serviceurl}}/analytics/kpis
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{access_token}}


###
GET {{serviceurl}}/lm/metrics
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{access_token}}

###
GET {{serviceurl}}/admin/dockerRegistrySecrets
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{access_token}}

###
GET {{serviceurl}}/lm/healthz
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{access_token}}


###
GET {{serviceurl}}/lm/artifacts
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{access_token}}

###
GET {{serviceurl}}/lm/articats/$count
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{access_token}}

###
GET {{serviceurl}}/lm/executions
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{access_token}}

###
GET {{serviceurl}}/lm/executions/$count
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{access_token}}

###
GET {{serviceurl}}/lm/deployments
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{access_token}}

###
GET {{serviceurl}}/lm/deployments/$count
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{access_token}}

###
GET {{serviceurl}}/lm/configurations
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{access_token}}

###
GET {{serviceurl}}/lm/configurations/$count
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{access_token}}

entry.title: Are there any APIs to access the Dashboards created in SAP Incentive Management Admin?
Delete variable assignment using API Incentive Management Admin
entry.content:
Hi,

Am trying to delete an assignment using API but the it throws an error. Is this expected to work? Can commission APIs used for deleting assignments?

We have different BU for position, different BU for position group and manager. Will delete operation work with different BU between these objects? 

PUT Body

[
    {
        "ruleElementOwnerSeq": "XXXXX",
        "effectiveStartDate": "3/19/2023",
        "effectiveEndDate": "1/1/2200",
        "variableAssignments": [
            {
                "owner": {"ruleElementOwnerSeq": "XXXXX"},
                "variable": {"ruleElementSeq": "YYYYY"},
                "assignment": {"ruleElementSeq": "ZZZZZ"}
            },
            {
                "owner": {"ruleElementOwnerSeq": "XXXXX"},
                "variable": {"ruleElementSeq": "AAAAA"},
                "assignment": null
            }
        ]
    }
]
 
Response
{
    "positions": {
        "0": {
            "manager": "TCMP_1001:E: Different business unit from businessUnitMap",
            "positionGroup": "TCMP_1001:E: Different business unit from businessUnitMap"
        }
    }
}

entry.title: Re: A very strange SQL requirement
entry.content:
Are you sure your requirement is correct?

There is no AF or BE flight on 30.05.2024, so what is the logic a line for these show up in the ALV? Just because another airline has a flight on that day?

entry.title: How to calculate Average on the Non-zero and Non-null values using Advanced Formulas
entry.content:
Dear SAC Experts,

Using Advanced Formulas, I would like to calculate the Average FTE value of the 'Actual' months and store the result in the the remaining 'Forecast' months.

The Actual & Forecast months are flagged by a property (FORECAST_FLAG) on the MONTH dimension.

Using the below code I was able to calculate it but I have hardcoded the number of Actual Months (to 10) and I would like to make it dynamic based on the number of Actual Months.

Result look like this:

Also, I would like to ignore the Zero & Null values and calculate average only based on the populated values, i.e. not always divide the total by the same number of months.

Could anyone of you please help with the code.

Thanks,

Sreekar

entry.title: Re: A very strange SQL requirement
entry.content: Imagine that these airlines are werks and the customer wants to know which have movements and which haven't. And this must be per day. This customer has this in his old system and he want it in SAP. I told you strange requirement.

entry.title: Re: Migration of Webi reports to Oracle analytics cloud
entry.content: developed from scratch

entry.title: Re: A very strange SQL requirement
entry.content:
According to the data in ZEK_SBOOK there is no movement of AR or BE on 30.05.24.

And: No, there is no SQL statement which invents data which is not in the table.

For hallucinating data I am afraid you have to use a LLM.

entry.content:
have you searched for “Duplicate Rule” in the SAP support notes before?
maybe some of these notes match your BO version !

entry.content:
your target folder has to be mapped/mounted in AL11,
so you can send your file also with OpenHub

entry.title: Re: Onboarding 1.0 Not Prompting for ID Information
entry.content: Known Defect, per KB I found: https://me.sap.com/notes/3476840/E

entry.title: Intercompany subledger clearing
entry.content:
Hello All,

I am working on requirement where, client is asking for clearing of Intercompany Customer & Vendors in automated manner. 

Example below, Inter Co Custer & Vendor should be in automated manner 

Dr. Vendor 1  $100; Cr. Inter Co Customer $100  [Company Code 001]

Dr. Expense   $100; Cr. Inter Co Vendor $100  [Company Code 099]

(1) Can you suggest SAP standard solutions for this requirement, how this can be automated? 

(2) Until the automated solution is in place, what can be manual solutions to clear existing line items, may be one time clearing (since there are huge entries, which business team is performing for manual clearing)

 

Thank you...

entry.title: Screenshot not working (Android)
entry.content:
Please need this for user concern/feedback. 

ios: working
android: not working

@Dan_Wroblewski 
@MaiaraEllwanger 
@marihirvi

entry.title: fiori configuration with logon group
entry.content:
Bearing in mind that our S/4HANA system has Fiori in the same instance, can you tell us how we should proceed to manage limiting executions to a logon group?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: fiori configuration with logon group
entry.content:
Dear customer,

I hope you're doing well.

 

Fiori doesn't support Logon Groups.

In case one wishes to enable Fiori only for certain groups of users, then only these users should have roles that contain Fiori content assigned to them, while users one does not desire to give Fiori access to should not have roles that include Fiori content assigned to them.

To know how to maintain roles for Fiori users, please read and follow the instructions in the documentation below:

  • SAP Help Portal - Assigning Users to Roles

 

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Can't post an invoice due to error message M8889? It's more OK than you think!
entry.content:
MIRO invoices are like airplanes: there is a whole checklist of security items to ensure that everything is properly set up to prevent risks and ensure that you're good to go.

However, it would be particularly frustrating if a pilot were simply not able to take off if an unknown error message simply showed up, moments before taking off!

SAP clerks may also face a similar frustration when trying to post invoices in MIRO and a sudden error message M8889 shows up moments before posting the invoice, when everything looked so correct!
MIRO invoices are generally not the case of life and death like planes, but financial impacts may also be a significant concern if the invoice is not posted in time.

So far, I have processed 20+ cases with exactly that same situation.
The good news is that that situation always has the exact same solution.

Short answer: The explanation and solution can be found in this KBA:
3277397 - MIRO: error message M8889 "Account XXXXXX has been set as not relevant for tax" - SAP ERP & SAP S/4HANA

However, if that KBA is too technical for you, or you are too nervous to get into details, fasten your seat belts and let's understand what's going on.

Although both planes and software pass through thorough security inspections, new problems may still be detected throughout time, even though the engineers thought that everything was properly covered!

But things like these happen... And something like that happened recently when SAP Note 3109666 was released.

That note basically says that no errors were being returned when a G/L account was set as not relevant for tax, and yet a posting line had that account and a tax code at the same time.
You may not agree, but that is actually a problem from SAP software perspective!
To fix that problem, program code was edited to trigger M8889 when it should always have been triggered!

So, if you suddenly started facing that error message, your SAP system had most probably gone through an update that included SAP Note 3109666.
And there is also no need to worry, your system is safer!

You might be asking now: "But it was OK to post that kind of invoice in the past, I just want to keep posting invoices that way!"
Well, I just said that there's no need to worry: simply set M8889 as a warning message in transaction code OMRM and then you will be available to keep posting invoices that way. The only difference will be the warning message.

By the way, all these images are from Plane Crazy (1928), featuring Mickey Mouse's first appearance.
That cartoon is entering public domain in 2024, so I guess there are no copyright infringements.
If the question is now solved for you, you may grab a cup of coffee and enjoy the short film (and try to find a possible Felix the Cat appearing nearly the end!).

entry.title: Re: How to calculate Average on the Non-zero and Non-null...
entry.content:
I got it working now.

Here's the code if anyone is interested.

Apparently I didn't need to deal with the NULL values particularly. The RESULTLOOKUP which populates the COUNT variable seems to ignore the null values by default.

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud Credit Management VKM4/VKM5 alternative solution?
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

May I know alternative solutions for VKM4/VKM5 in SAP public cloud? Because in DCD (documented credit decision) not show blocked delivery.

We have business requirements for credit management, only credit block for delivery level not for sales order level.

Thanks.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS1 Release: Project Manufacturing Management and Optimization – What’s New
entry.content:
SAP Project Manufacturing Management and Optimization (PMMO) helps achieve greater efficiency with project manufacturing in an Engineer to Order (ETO) environment to deliver complex end products (for example: military aircrafts, ships and submarines, complex industrial machinery etc.), that often involves complex engineering and production processes.

In this blog post, we'll take a look at what's new in Project Manufacturing Management and Optimization (PMMO) in S/4HANA 2023 FPS1 Release. I also encourage you to check out my other blog posts to take a look at some of the key features available in previous releases.

Key Features in S/4HANA 2023 FPS1 Release

Let's take a look at the key highlights from 2023 FPS1 release in PMMO:

Settlement of PMMO relevant Production Orders

In my previous blog post, I had written about an enhancement that can be used to prevent PMMO relevant Manufacturing Production Orders (those that are account assigned to a Grouping WBS element) being settled through normal Order Settlement. With 2023 FPS1, there is no need for this enhancement, since a newly added configuration within PMMO now makes this possible without having the need for an enhancement. 

Within the IMG node "Configure Client-Specific settings for Distribution", you can now specify a Settlement Profile. This Settlement Profile will be automatically used within the Order Settlement for any Production Order that is account assigned to a Grouping WBS (both Type 1 and Type 2 Grouping). The settlement profile should be configured via Transaction OK07 as "Not for Settlement".

Settlement Profile configuration in OK07

Settlement Profile entry in PMMO Configuration

By adding this Settlement Profile within PMMO configuration, all Production Orders (Order Category '10') that are account assigned to a Grouping WBS will be settled only through PMMO Cost Distribution and not through Order Settlement.

Excel upload for Exceptions Master Data

Similar to the excel upload feature that was available for Breakpoints in 2023 FPS0 release, this feature allows using an excel upload for Exceptions Master Data. The excel template depends on the Exception Handling Category. This feature uses the Function Module API PMMO_MAINTAIN_PEG_EXCEPTIONS, which performs the checks before data is inserted, updated or deleted.

Transaction PMMO_EXCEPTIONS_UPL

Breakpoint Hierarchy

Breakpoints are used in PMMO to build a cost structure that differs from the production structure. Breakpoints allow costs to be redirected from individual requirement operative WBS to another cost object (Network Activity, WBS or Cost Center). In a project structure, an operative WBS can have various other operative WBS as lower-level nodes. With 2023 FPS1, Breakpoints can be set at higher level WBS structure with a hierarchy flag. When this flag is set, Pegging will determine all the lower level WBS nodes and apply the target breakpoint. This greatly reduces the number of Breakpoint records that need to be maintained in the Breakpoint table. 

As you can see from the below example, the hierarchy flag provides the capability to set the breakpoint at any WBS element in the Contract hierarchy and reduces the number of entries needed to be maintained in the breakpoint table. An entry without the hierarchy flag set takes higher precedence over an entry with the hierarchy flag set.

As can be seen in the above example, the breakpoint entries need not be specified for every single WBS element, instead a higher level WBS Element in the hierarchy can be used to set the breakpoint and any WBS Elements that are added below this WBS Element would automatically be included during the Pegging Breakpoint processing.

The Pegging program applies the breakpoint respecting the hierarchy and the standard breakpoint processing logic.

The breakpoint master data excel upload utility (PMMO_BREAKPOINT_UPL) has also been extended to support the hierarchy indicator. 

Distribution Balance Check enhancements

PMMO Distribution Balance Check (Transaction PMMO_DISTR_BALCHK) can help analyze undistributed balance on Group WBS after the PMMO Distribution run. In a perfect world, the balance on the Group WBS after the PMMO Distribution run should be 0. However, there are cases where the balance on the Group is not 0. This can be due to Pegging errors, costs on Group WBS that PMMO cannot distribute etc. In S/4HANA 2023 FPS1, the Distribution Balance Check has been enhanced to provide additional information in the output including MRP Order Qty, MRP Received Qty, PMMO Received Qty, PMMO Assigned Quantity, Period and Year that would help the user analyzing the results of the balance check. In addition, the results can be optionally saved and displayed when executing the Balance Check. Since the results can be persisted, customers can set thresholds for balances during period end closing to identify balances on the Grouping WBSs and Exception WBSs to decide whether month end closing should continue. The report also includes actual cost balances on the Default Exception WBSs (that are included in Transaction PMMO_PEGEXC) for Excess, Scrap, Loss and Underpeg costs that could not be allocated to the financial WBS elements.

Note that you can also use the standard PS reports like CJI3 to check balances on the Grouping WBS and Default Exception WBS Elements. 

Summary

I hope this blog post keeps you up to date on the latest innovations in PMMO. Stay tuned for the next blog post on the latest features that will be available in PMMO in S/4HANA 2023 FPS2. As always, feedback and comments welcome!

entry.title: Re: "Text Through Text Associations" in RAP triggers exce...
entry.content:
I faced the same issue. To resolve, I exposed the  reference Text element in same projection view . Finally used @ObjectModel.text.element instead of @ObjectModel.text.assocation.

Hope that will resolve the issue. p.s. As per SAP Help documentation "You cannot combine text associations with projection scenarios."

entry.title: Re: Which Module in SAP should I choose
entry.content:
hello,

I am also in similar situation not able to decide whether to go for it or not?

After 3.6yrs working in Sap abap + Fica domain also now i am having few offers in SAP MDG is one of them and one offer is from Startup which is 4 yrs old. Not able to decide which to join. Can you please help me

entry.title: SLT Servers High space / memory utilization
entry.content:
Table ODQDATA (Operational Delta queue) have grown nearly 300 GB.

ODQ_CLEANUP job has been running but the time is very long (> 1 day) which lead to timeout issue.

How to make ODQ_CLEANUP job finishes successfully to reduce the number of table ODQDATA?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SLT Servers High space / memory utilization
entry.content:
1. Make sure no old data in ODQMON and all obsolete subscriptions have been deleted. See note 2502498 - Huge amount of data in ODQDATA table
  2. Follow note 2300483 - ODQ - performance of ODQDATA_V and ODQTSNLOG to create indexes:
    • Secondary index on the table ODQDATA: Fields: CLIENT, MODELNAME, QUEUENAME
    • Secondary index on the table ODQTSNLOG: CLIENT, TID

  3. Apply note 3213659 - ODQ_CLEANUP: Runtime error SYSTEM_NO_ROLL in delete_queue_data

  4. Execute ODQ_CLEANUP job with different values of Retention period to clear the data one period by one period
    [For example]: Suppose that there're old data over than 20 months:
    • Delete them "month-wise" starting with a retention time of 19 months (24h * 30 days/month * 19 months = 13,680 hours to start with):
      Recovery  13,680 Hours  <-----
      Data with low relevance 10 Days
      Data with average relevance 31 Days

      If this is successful it should have deleted confirmed data older than 19 months.

    • Change the variant always reducing the retention time by 720 hours (a month with 30 days):
      Recovery  12,960 Hours  <----- 13,680 - 720
      Data with low relevance 10 Days
      Data with average relevance 31 Days

    • Next retention time should be reduced step by step from 12,240, 11,520, 10,800, ... , to 720 for the last month finally.

entry.title: Re: How to select a specific session to do SAP GUI Scripting activities inside it
entry.content:
Hello @ndionisio_giv,

thank you very much for your comment and your kind words.

First of all congratulations that you decided to getting involved with programming. This is a good way to effectively realize your own automations.

You have detect a very interesting behavior that I have never noticed before. A session that is being debugged blocks the call of connection.Children(CLng(j)) - in your case in line 55 - to get the session. I tried it with dotNET and here it is the same behavior. Scripting Tracker works on a completely different basis that cannot be compared with these approaches. I will think about how this problem can possibly be solved, but I don't want to promise a solution.

See you later, best regards
Stefan

entry.title: SAP Joule
entry.content: Do we have any trail version of Joule. Can we do a POC

entry.title: Re: Coupons not showing in SAP Customer Checkout
entry.content:
Solved.

I synchronized coupons using "Discount" under "Synchronization" in tab in Customer checkout POS
